behavior_summary: This script implements a simple in-world candelabra item with three process handlers: a U8P_CANDLBRA::look handler that displays the item's short label, an U8P_CANDLBRA::anim helper that triggers a visual animation change, and a U8P_CANDLBRA::use handler that toggles or advances the item's visual frame bits to change its visible state.
U8P_CANDLBRA::look: when the player examines the object it emits a single bark text 'candelabra' and then suspends/waits.
U8P_CANDLBRA::anim: invokes the engine helper METHOD::reverseAnimFrame(this) to step or reverse the item's displayed animation and then suspends.

U8P_CANDLBRA::use: reads the item's current frame (via Item::getFrame(this)), inspects the low two bits and updates the frame (via Item::setFrame): if (frame & 0x03) == 0 then frame += 1 (advance a low-state); otherwise clear the low two bits (frame &= 0x00FC), effectively resetting that lower-state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CANDLBRA::look: linear — show bark 'candelabra' then suspend (no branching).
U8P_CANDLBRA::anim: linear — calls METHOD::reverseAnimFrame(this) then suspend.
U8P_CANDLBRA::use: contains a single conditional based on the item's frame low two bits: branch A when (frame & 0x03) == 0 (increment low-state), branch B otherwise (clear low two bits).
open_questions: What exact game-state meaning do the low two frame bits encode (e.g., lit/unlit, flicker stages, or number of candles)? (inferred only from bitwise ops).
What engine or script action triggers U8P_CANDLBRA::anim in normal gameplay (timed event, explicit call from another script, or called internally after U8P_CANDLBRA::use)?
Does U8P_CANDLBRA::use also intend to call U8P_CANDLBRA::anim after changing the frame to produce a visible transition, or are animation and frame updates decoupled?

main_function.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::look
main_function.purpose: Display the short in-world description when the player examines the candelabra (player-facing label).
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines this item (U8P_CANDLBRA::look/examine event).
Behavior: present the bark text 'candelabra' and then suspend/end the U8P_CANDLBRA::look process.

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::anim
function_map.role: drive visual animation frame change via engine helper

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::use
function_map.role: handle player activation: read and update the item's frame bits to toggle/advance visual state

external_calls.purpose: Reverse or step the displayed animation frame for the item (visual effect).
Return the current frame/index for the item so the script can inspect low bits and decide state transition.
Set the item's frame/index to the new value computed by the U8P_CANDLBRA::use handler (apply state change).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a simple in-world candelabra item with three process handlers: a U8P_CANDLBRA::look handler that displays the item's short label, an U8P_CANDLBRA::anim helper that triggers a visual animation change, and a U8P_CANDLBRA::use handler that toggles or advances the item's visual frame bits to change its visible state.
